extra.reasoning: The property requires NOT(rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b) for all inputs. rec_drug_b is set true only by r1 (IF g6pd_deficiency). rec_drug_a is set true only by r4, whose conjunction includes g6pd_deficiency along with 9 other conditions. Therefore any input that fires r4 (making rec_drug_a true) necessarily has g6pd_deficiency true, which also fires r1 (making rec_drug_b true). Since r1 is the only rule for rec_drug_b and r4 is the only rule for rec_drug_a, and priorities are per-output (no cross-output override), both outputs end up true simultaneously. Setting all 10 conjuncts of r4 to true (g6pd_deficiency, renal_impairment, anticoagulated, pregnancy, prior_gi_bleed, heart_failure, bradycardia, peptic_ulcer, diabetes, elderly) yields rec_drug_a=true (via r4) and rec_drug_b=true (via r1). hyperkalemia and hypertension are irrelevant to a/b and set false. This jointly satisfies rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b, violating mutual exclusion.
